prior_year_terms.promo_commitment: 4 major sales per year at 25% off

thrive_asks: Instock guarantee of 92%+ with penalty clause
6 new items including 2 GLP-1 optimized SKUs
30-day exclusive on all new item launches
Reduce lead time from 14 days to 10 days
vendor_asks: Prominent homepage placement during grilling season
Access to Thrive member data for product development

outcomes.payment_terms: Stayed at Net 30, vendor withdrew Net 45 ask
outcomes.promo_commitment: 5 sales at 25% off (increased from 4, vendor accepted)
outcomes.instock_guarantee: Soft commitment to 90%+, no penalty clause (Thrive wanted 92% with teeth)
outcomes.exclusivity: 14-day exclusive on new items (compromise from 30-day ask)
outcomes.vendor_wins: Got premium placement during July grilling feature, limited member survey data sharing

negotiation_records.relationship_notes: Strong partnership but Primal Provisions knows they're our largest meat vendor and uses that leverage. Derek is a skilled negotiator -- comes prepared with competitive data. Lisa is more collaborative and open to creative deal structures. Lead time remains a pain point -- their Montana facility is far from our distribution centers. Fill rate (84.2%) is genuinely concerning and they acknowledged it's a capacity issue. They're expanding production in Q3 2026 which should help. Watch: they're also selling to Amazon Fresh now, which gives them an alternative channel argument.
unresolved_issues: Fill rate still well below benchmark at 84.2%
Lead time at 12 days, was supposed to improve to 10
Instock guarantee has no enforcement mechanism
Co-marketing spend came in at $32K actual (under the $35K commitment)

prior_year_terms.promo_commitment: 6 major sales per year at 20-30% off

thrive_asks: Launch GLP-1 support supplement line (3 SKUs) exclusively on Thrive
Increase co-marketing to $60K with focus on influencer partnerships
Formalize instock guarantee with penalty clause
vendor_asks: Feature placement in Thrive's email newsletter (4x per year)
Inclusion in Thrive's subscription/auto-ship program for top 5 SKUs
Access to Thrive customer review data for R&D

outcomes.margin: Agreed to 52.1% (strong win, Amanda values the Thrive relationship highly)
outcomes.instock_guarantee: 97%+ maintained, still no formal penalty (Amanda: 'our track record speaks for itself')
outcomes.new_item_pipeline: 8 items including 3 GLP-1 support SKUs (exceeded ask)
outcomes.exclusivity: 5 Thrive-exclusive SKUs including 2 GLP-1 items (big win)
outcomes.vendor_wins: Got newsletter features (4x), auto-ship inclusion for top 3 SKUs, anonymized review themes shared quarterly

negotiation_records.relationship_notes: Suncoast is our model vendor partnership. Amanda (CEO) treats Thrive as a strategic channel, not just a customer. She's genuinely invested in co-creating products for our member base. Brian runs the numbers and is fair -- he'll push on margin but accepts data-driven arguments. The GLP-1 exclusive line is a potential breakout -- if it performs, this could be a $8M+ relationship within 18 months. Key risk: Suncoast is growing fast and getting attention from Target and Walmart natural sections. If they go mass market, our exclusive positioning erodes. Need to lock in deeper exclusivity this cycle.
unresolved_issues: Return rate at 1.8% -- slightly above their 1.5% ask, but below our 2.1% platform average
Target reportedly approached them for an exclusive wellness line
Co-marketing actual spend was $48K (close to $50K commitment, minor shortfall)
Want to discuss whether subscription pricing should differ from one-time purchase

thrive_asks: Increase new item pipeline to 4 items with at least 1 low-sugar/GLP-1 friendly option
Improve fill rate to 92%+ (current 88.6% is below benchmark)
Reduce lead time from 8 days to 6 days
vendor_asks: Guaranteed shelf space (minimum 6 SKUs always active)
Advance notice on promotional calendar (60 days)

outcomes.margin: Agreed to 32.1% (partial win -- Tom argued raw ingredient costs up 8%)
outcomes.promo_commitment: 4 sales maintained at 20% off, added 1 additional sale at 15% off
outcomes.instock_guarantee: Verbal commitment to 93%+, no formal mechanism
outcomes.new_item_pipeline: 3 items (compromise from 4), no GLP-1 specific commitment
outcomes.fill_rate: Tom committed to 'addressing supply chain issues' -- no specific target
outcomes.lead_time: No improvement committed, cited supplier constraints
outcomes.vendor_wins: Got founder story feature, 45-day advance promo notice (compromise from 60)

negotiation_records.relationship_notes: GreenVale is a beloved member brand (high review ratings, loyal repeat buyers) but operationally frustrating. Tom is a passionate founder who makes great product but runs a lean operation that can't always keep up with demand. Rachel is more commercially savvy and understands our concerns, but doesn't have authority to commit to operational changes without Tom. The fill rate and instock issues are real and getting worse -- three stockouts in Q4 that hurt our member experience. Tom blames supply chain; our ops team thinks it's production planning. The brand has goodwill with our members but we can't keep apologizing for out-of-stocks. This JBP needs to be firmer -- specific improvement targets with consequences.
unresolved_issues: Fill rate actually declined from 90.1% to 88.6% since last JBP (going the wrong direction)
Instock rate also declined from 92.8% to 91.2%
3 new items launched instead of promised 3 -- but none were innovative, just line extensions
Co-marketing spend was exactly $12K but low effort (generic social posts, not custom Thrive content)
No progress on GLP-1 or low-sugar product development despite being a clear market trend
Return rate at 2.8% is significantly above platform average -- quality consistency issue?
